# Transcrição: 9. Tips on using Information to Build Trust

- **Fonte:** 9. Tips on using Information to Build Trust
- **Duração:** 03:14
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

## [00:00 - 03:00]

So, here's some very specific tips on information to build trust. Never sell with words, only show documentation. I know I'm pounding this in like a man we're talking about trust again. Trust is vital to a sale. Number two, never negotiate with words, write your negotiations down on paper. Three, never ask for the close with words, use a buyer's order. Sir, notice what I have here? Okay, use that. Not, hey, what do you think? Let's do it. Most people are talking, not closing. Four, never make verbal promises. Put your assurances in writing. There's a guarantee, a warranty, some promise, put it in writing. Otherwise, they're not going to trust it. Number five, the more data, the better. Less data is not better. More data is better. Don't be afraid to use lots of data. Doesn't mean they're going to look at it. Doesn't mean they even care about it. Be willing to provide it. Six, keep your information current. Something from a year ago means nothing to the guy today. Okay? Seven, have your written information available and easy to access. Even pre-built if you could, okay? Maybe for people calling in, people hitting your website, package out to them with information. Eight, I think I'm on eight. Use third-party data as much as possible. Third-party references, other sources, credible sources other than yourself. Number nine, the more you're able to access data in real time, the better. Okay? Now, there's an exception to this because if you could have a prepared package and pop it out, that's good. Prepared package is not bad, but if a guy could look on the net with you in real time, oh my gosh, that's just going to like wham, crunch. Okay? Ten, use computer-generated data whenever possible. What internet access available to you right now in the moment? Okay? In front of the customer so they can see that it's not contrived, it's not manipulated, and they can trust it. So make it easy for the buyer to do research while they're with you, not later at home. Do it now. Get it handled. Remember, if they have enough information, it'll build trust and they can make a decision. If the buyer wants to offer their own information, research it. Look at it with them. Encourage them to do so. Don't back off from that. After consulting hundreds, probably thousands of companies, okay, like we have 400 clients on retainer where we're in their operation every 60 days. On the phone with them every two weeks, after consulting hundreds of companies on improving sales processes, I've often encouraged the business management and the salespeople, okay, to make all competitive advertising available and fully displayed in their offices so that the buyer doesn't have to go out and look at competition. You see what I'm saying? I would actually have all my competitors, what they offer, you know, the products they offer, the warranties, the services they offer, and their prices. Why have them shop? This is being used by mass retailers today, okay?

## [03:00 - 03:14]

So what are you going to do out of this? Go over those 10. They're critical. Work on them. Put them into your business. Get that set up to build credibility and you will see people make decisions with you now when they used to be making decisions with you later.
